author: Andrea Barberio
message:
[go] Update go-graphviz to v0.2.10 and migrate ToDOT

v0.2 is a breaking API change (context-based New/Render, Create*ByName, Node.Name returns an error); migrate results.ToDOT(). It also drops cgo for a pure-Go WASM backend, so the module builds with CGO_ENABLED=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrea Barberio <insomniac@slackware.it>

author: Andrea Barberio
message:
[go] Clamp negative RTT to avoid a uint64 underflow

On fast paths (e.g. routest loopback) the reply can be timestamped before the send, making received-sent negative; clamp it to zero before the uint64 conversion in the v4 and v6 probes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrea Barberio <insomniac@slackware.it>

author: Andrea Barberio
message:
[go] Update Go dependencies

Refresh all dependencies via 'go get -u ./...' + 'go mod tidy': go-nfqueue v1.3.1->v1.3.2, go-graphviz v0.1.2->v0.1.3, logrus v1.9.3->v1.9.4, pflag v1.0.5->v1.0.10, testify v1.8.2->v1.10.0, golang.org/x/net v0.38.0->v0.55.0, golang.org/x/sys v0.31.0->v0.45.0, plus the indirect dependencies.

go-graphviz is held on the v0.1.x line: v0.2.x is a breaking change (context-based API, cgraph Node.Name() now returns an error, and a switch to a pure-Go WASM backend) that requires migrating results.ToDOT(); that is left for a separate change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrea Barberio <insomniac@slackware.it>

author: Andrea Barberio
message:
[go] Build routest only on Linux

routest depends on Linux-only facilities -- NFQUEUE (via go-nfqueue and netlink) and unix.BindToDevice (SO_BINDTODEVICE) -- so it cannot build on macOS, where it failed with 'cmd/routest/send.go: undefined: unix.BindToDevice'. Add a //go:build linux constraint to the package's files; with the ./... wildcard, go build/vet/test/list silently skip packages whose files are all excluded for the target platform, so the macOS CI jobs no longer compile routest while the Linux integ build is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrea Barberio <insomniac@slackware.it>

author: Andrea Barberio
message:
[go] Fix broken net unit tests and run the suite in CI

The net and probev6 test packages no longer built/vetted: net/icmp_test.go called the misspelled UnmarshalBinaryBinary; udp_test.go and ipv6_test.go used the pre-refactor API (the removed IPv4 type, UDP.SetPrev, and SetNext on a *UDP, which is no longer a Layer); and probev6/udpv6.go used the %w verb in log.Printf. Update the tests to the current net API (UDP.PseudoHeader for checksums, Raw payload chaining) and switch the log verb to %v.

Also fix the test_go CI job, which ran 'go list ./...' from cmd/dublin-traceroute and therefore only matched the CLI main package (no test files), so the real test packages were never exercised. Run it from the module root instead.

Bump the Go version to 1.26.3 in go.mod and across the Tests workflow (build/test matrices and the C++ and integ jobs), replacing the previous 1.21/1.22 pins.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrea Barberio <insomniac@slackware.it>

author: Andrea Barberio
message:
[go] Remove dead net/ipv4.go and its stale test

The body of net/ipv4.go was entirely commented out in 06d4c88 ("[go] replace ipv6/ipv6 network library with golang.org/x/net"), when the local IPv4 header implementation was superseded by golang.org/x/net/ipv4. The file has defined nothing since, but net/ipv4_test.go still referenced the removed types (IPv4, DontFragment, Option, Version4, ...) and broke 'go test' for the net package. No non-test code uses the local net.IPv4 type, and the still-needed IPv4HeaderToPseudoHeader helper lives in net/udp.go.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrea Barberio <insomniac@slackware.it>

author: Andrea Barberio
message:
Fix several crashes in the C++ implementation

- udpv4probe.cc: build the probe RawPDU from an explicit [begin, end)
  byte range instead of `RawPDU((char *)payload)`. The bare char* selected
  the std::string(const char*) constructor, which runs strlen over the
  buffer; since the trailing identifier bytes are usually non-zero there is
  no NUL terminator, so it read past the end of the stack array (confirmed
  stack-buffer-overflow under AddressSanitizer).

- dublin_traceroute.cc: always stop and join the ICMP listener thread before
  traceroute() returns. Previously any exception thrown after the thread was
  created (e.g. a failed probe send / no route to host) unwound past a still
  joinable std::thread, calling std::terminate() and aborting the process.
  A ListenerGuard RAII object now joins the thread on every exit path, and an
  atomic stop flag lets the error path tear down promptly.

- dublin_traceroute.cc: hold mutex_tracerouting with a std::unique_lock
  (try_to_lock) instead of a manual try_lock()/unlock(). The manual unlock()
  was skipped on the exception path, leaving the mutex locked and deadlocking
  the destructor; also close(sock) before throwing on setsockopt failure.

- dublin_traceroute.cc: replace the uninitialized msg_control pointer with a
  properly sized control buffer so recvmsg no longer references garbage (and
  SO_TIMESTAMP kernel timestamps now actually work), and use ssize_t for the
  recvmsg return value.

- hop.cc, traceroute_results.cc: require a full 4-byte entry before parsing an
  MPLS label stack entry, so a malformed (non-multiple-of-4) ICMP MPLS
  extension from the network cannot cause an out-of-bounds vector read. Also
  remove dead code in TracerouteResults::show() that re-parsed the inner IP
  and could throw an uncaught exception.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrea Barberio <insomniac@slackware.it>

author: Andrea Barberio
message:
[go] replace ipv6/ipv6 network library with golang.org/x/net

Go's x/net/ipv{6,4} packages provide fewer and more basic facilities for
packet handling, but their portability is better than the embedded `net`
library in dublin-traceroute. For better portability I am replacing the
ipv6 and ipv4 layers with Go's ones, while leaving UDP and ICMP handling
in the embedded net library.

Signed-off-by: Andrea Barberio <insomniac@slackware.it>
